title: Mighty Syrian Lamb Pie
description: A spicy, chewy lamb pie brushed with a shortcrust pastry, perfect for feeding a crowd and sugar‑free friendly.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and roll out the shortcrust pastry to fit a 23cm pie dish.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and brown the lamb cubes until a crust forms, about 7 minutes.
Add the onions, garlic, and red bell pepper to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the cumin, paprika, coriander, cayenne pepper, cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves, cooking for 2 minutes to release aromas.
Mix in the tomato paste and a splash of water, then reduce heat to low and simmer the filling for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season the mixture with salt and let it cool slightly.
Transfer the lamb filling into the prepared pastry crust, smoothing the top.
Brush the edges of the crust with beaten egg to seal.
Cover the pie with a second layer of pastry, crimping the edges, and cut slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pie for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.
Let the pie rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
